front: Novel: shepherd Santiago travels across the Sahara to find treasure buried under the Egyptian Pyramids, only to discover it was back in Andalusia where he started
Novel: the King of Salem, Melchizedek, gives the protagonist the divination stones Urim and Thummim
Novel: after being robbed of his savings, Santiago works for a crystal merchant in Tangier before joining a caravan across the Sahara
Novel: its prologue retells the myth of Narcissus — the lake mourns because it can no longer see its own beauty reflected in his eyes
Novel: treasure is ultimately found buried under a sycamore tree outside an abandoned church in Andalusia
Novel: the protagonist sees one hawk strike another, triggering a vision of his oasis coming under siege; later he escapes desert militants by turning himself into the wind
Novel: shares its title with a Ben Jonson comedy featuring con men Face and Subtle who trick Dapper into consulting the Queen of Fairy
Novel: a character writes 'The Emerald Tablet is a direct passage to the Soul of the World' in the sand
Novel: contains the quote 'If you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you achieve it'
Novel: features a depressed Slovenian woman named Veronika who attempts suicide and is committed to a mental institution
Author: Brazilian novelist who wrote in Portuguese and is frequently paired with Jorge Amado as a representative of Brazilian literature
Author: his central spiritual concept is the 'Personal Legend' — each person's individual destiny that the universe helps them achieve
